commands.title: SNMP: Basic walk (v2c, community)
commands.description: Perform a full SNMP walk using SNMPv2c and a community string (e.g., public).
commands.notes: Use `-On` to show numeric OIDs; use `-r`/`-t` to tune retries/timeouts.

commands.title: SNMP: Bulk walk (faster for v2c)
commands.description: Use Bulkwalk to pull large SNMP tables faster (v2c). Replace OID with desired tree.

commands.title: SNMP: Get a single OID (v1/2c)
commands.description: Query a specific OID (useful for targeted checks like sysName, sysDescr, sysContact).

commands.title: SNMP: Set an OID (test write access)
commands.description: Attempt to write to a writable OID (requires write community). Use cautiously and only in-scope.

commands.title: SNMP: Extract common system info (sysDescr/sysName/sysLocation)
commands.template: snmpwalk -v2c -c {community} {ip} SNMPv2-MIB::sysDescr.0 SNMPv2-MIB::sysName.0 SNMPv2-MIB::sysLocation.0 | tee -a snmp_sysinfo.txt
commands.description: Quickly capture basic host info and save to a file.

commands.title: SNMP: Try common public-like communities (onesixtyone)
commands.description: Fast scanner/bruter for common community strings (supply a wordlist).
commands.notes: Onesixtyone is faster than naive brute-forcing; tune with a small wordlist of likely names.

commands.title: SNMP: Brute-force community strings (mass bruteforce example)
commands.template: for c in $(cat {wordlist}); do snmpget -v2c -c $c {ip} SNMPv2-MIB::sysDescr.0 && echo "FOUND:$c" && break; done
commands.description: Simple bash loop that tries community strings from a list and stops on success.
commands.notes: Replace `{wordlist}` with your community wordlist file.

commands.title: SNMP: Enumerate interfaces (IF-MIB)
commands.description: Enumerate network interfaces and their names for pivoting or mapping.

commands.title: SNMP: ARP table (ipNetToMedia or ipNetToPhysical)
commands.description: Try common OID trees to extract ARP/neighbor tables (useful for network mapping).

commands.title: snmp-check: automated SNMP enumeration
commands.description: Run snmp-check (python tool) for automated discovery of useful SNMP info (users, config, MIBs).
commands.notes: snmp-check is opinionated — good quick summary of interesting OIDs.

commands.title: snmptranslate: convert OID names ↔ numbers
commands.description: Translate between textual OID names and numeric OIDs; useful when reading MIB output.

commands.title: snmpwalk numeric OIDs and grep for admin strings
commands.description: Scan SNMP output for human-readable hints like usernames, community strings, contact info, or secrets.

commands.title: SNMP: Enumerate via SNMPv3 (authPriv example)
commands.description: Query SNMPv3-enabled targets using authentication and privacy (AES/DES).

commands.title: SNMP: Save full walk to file for offline analysis
commands.description: Store raw output for later parsing or inclusion in reports.

commands.title: SMTP: Send test email (auth PLAIN) using swaks
commands.description: Send a simple test email via swaks with PLAIN auth (useful to validate SMTP credentials).
commands.notes: Wrap passwords with single quotes if they contain special characters or spaces.

commands.title: SMTP: Send email with attachment via swaks
commands.description: Send an email with an attached file (e.g., notice.doc) using swaks.
commands.notes: Use absolute path for `{file}` if not in current directory.
